title: Sammlung altenglischer Legenden
summary: The pinned General Corpus XML serializes U-M electronic item AJD8171.0001.001, an uncorrected-OCR/TEI representation of Carl Horstmann's 1878 eighteen-component anthology. Horstmann transcribed or collated fifteen physical manuscript witnesses, sometimes through Laing's print or working transcripts by Lucy Toulmin Smith, Eugen Kölbing, and F. J. Furnivall. Four components print two version streams. This graph keeps the unavailable sixteenth manuscript reference (CUL Ii.4.9), U-M page-image production, the collateral Boston Public Library scan, complete manuscript facsimiles, detached Auchinleck fragments, catalogs, rights statements, and repository artifact distinct.
review_notes: The cataloging subject is the complete 1878 edited selection: eighteen count-bearing components and twenty-two serialized streams.
No abstract-work entity is minted for each component; component identity and metrics live in work metadata, while relations are scoped to component labels and exact ranges.
Direct and mediated editorial routes are separated. No direct Horstmann-to-codex edge is asserted where Laing or a working transcript supplied the reading.
CUL Ii.4.9 is retained only to resolve the printed Cbr. Ji IV, 8 reference and is explicitly excluded from the 1878 source chain because Horstmann says it was unavailable.

Every cached object states target-work presence. The eight approved representation groups account for ten present local copies; the other twenty-seven local copies are absent and have no work_portion.
The older Auchinleck project is a 701-image reconstruction spanning the NLS codex and detached Edinburgh fragments, not one fictional physical carrier.
No death date is asserted for the correct Carl Horstmann authority.

entities.label: Horstmann, Sammlung altenglischer Legenden (1878)
entities.description: The immediate scholarly source: seven Vernon-derived components followed by eleven individual legends and an appendix, with German apparatus and Latin comparison material.

bibliographic.citation: Carl Horstmann, ed., Sammlung altenglischer Legenden, grösstentheils zum ersten Male herausgegeben (Heilbronn: Gebr. Henninger, 1878).
bibliographic.title: Sammlung altenglischer Legenden

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of the Boston Public Library Horstmann 1878 carrier
entities.description: A 2010 scan of a Boston Public Library copy. It is collateral evidence and is not identified as the physical carrier behind U-M's page images.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF, OCR, scandata, and complete JP2 image ZIP

entities.label: Grässe, Legenda aurea (1846)
entities.description: The Latin comparison edition Horstmann cites for the seven Part I chapters; it is comparison material, not the Middle English copy-text.

bibliographic.citation: Theodor Grässe, ed., Jacobi a Voragine Legenda aurea, vulgo Historia Lombardica dicta (1846).
bibliographic.title: Legenda aurea, vulgo Historia Lombardica dicta

entities.label: Laing, A Penni Worth of Witte (1857)
entities.description: The printed intermediary from which Horstmann took Canticum de Creatione Auchinleck-version lines 1–352.

bibliographic.citation: David Laing, ed., A Penni Worth of Witte: Florice and Blauncheflour and Other Pieces of Ancient English Poetry, Selected from the Auchinleck Manuscript (Edinburgh: Abbotsford Club, 1857).
bibliographic.title: A Penni Worth of Witte

entities.label: Vernon Manuscript

entities.label: Lucy Toulmin Smith working transcripts of Auchinleck fragments
entities.description: Modern handwritten working copies used by Horstmann for Canticum lines 353–end and the second Magdalena version. The schema type manuscript_witness is provisional for a research transcript, not a claim that it is medieval.
notes: Current repository and survival status were not established.

entities.label: Eugen Kölbing working transcript of the Auchinleck Magdalena
entities.description: A modern working transcript Horstmann says he consulted for the second Magdalena version. The schema type manuscript_witness is provisional for a research transcript.
notes: Current repository and survival status were not established.

entities.label: F. J. Furnivall working transcript of the Vernon Adam-and-Eve text
entities.description: The modern working transcript through which Horstmann received the appendix at Vernon f. 393r–v. The schema type manuscript_witness is provisional for a research transcript.
notes: Current repository and survival status were not established.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A multi-witness body of Middle English legendary, devotional, romance, and prose material.
editorial_practices.representation: An eighteen-component anthology arranged as seven Vernon-derived Part I legends and eleven Part II components, with four components presenting two separately serialized versions.
editorial_practices.description: Catalog the volume as an edited selection, not as a single medieval work and not as twenty-two count-bearing works. The four dual-version components count once each at the parent level; their eight child streams own the line and group metrics.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The scanned edition includes title/dedication matter and an unnumbered contents page outside the eighteen textual components.
editorial_practices.representation: The U-M/CME encoding serializes those surfaces, but the work-metadata record treats them as representation-only rather than count-bearing items.
editorial_practices.description: Preserve the full electronic representation while excluding dedication and contents from item, form, and line/group totals.
editorial_practices.scope: Front matter and terminal contents surface

editorial_practices.source_feature: Dorothe survives in Harley MS 5272 and Arundel MS 168 with verse and surrounding Latin/prayer material.
editorial_practices.representation: Harley MS 5272 supplies the principal text, Arundel MS 168 is collated, and Arundel supplies final lines 335–344.
editorial_practices.description: Model the principal transcription, general collation, and supplied ending as separate scoped relations.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Multiple manuscript versions of Roberd of Cisyle.
editorial_practices.representation: An eclectic printed text draws principally on Vernon and Trinity College Oxford MS 57 and collates Harley MSS 1701 and 525 plus CUL MS Ff.2.38.
editorial_practices.description: Retain two principal transcription edges and three collation edges; do not assign an unsupported exact line partition between Vernon and Trinity.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Auchinleck and Vernon readings reached Horstmann through Laing's 1857 edition or working transcripts by Lucy Toulmin Smith, Eugen Kölbing, and F. J. Furnivall.
editorial_practices.representation: Each intermediary is an explicit node, with separate intermediary-to-physical-witness relations where the route is supported.
editorial_practices.description: Do not flatten mediated transcription into direct edition-to-codex claims. Current repositories for the three working transcripts remain unresolved.
editorial_practices.scope: Canticum version b, Magdalena version b, and the Adam-and-Eve appendix

evidence.citation: Kyvero Press, CME ajd8171.xml, pinned at Git commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: Complete file; IDNO AJD8171.0001.001; HEADER, FRONT, BODY, and BACK.
evidence.summary: Pins the repository artifact, U-M source description, 2006 OCR/TEI statement, legacy availability statement, image count, and complete serialized text.

evidence.citation: Structural and metric inspection of the pinned CME ajd8171 XML.
evidence.locator: Count-bearing DIV hierarchy, verse line elements, prose paragraphs, dedication, and contents.
evidence.summary: Reconciles eighteen count-bearing components, twenty-two serialized streams, eight dual-version child streams, 670 line groups, 13,275 verse lines, and 15 prose paragraphs; dedication and contents are representation-only.

evidence.citation: Kyvero Press CME repository LICENSE, pinned at Git commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Contains Unlicense language framed around software; it does not expressly settle whether repository XML/data are included.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, AJD8171.0001.001.
evidence.locator: Bibliographic record, encoding statement, access links, and current rights statement.
evidence.summary: Identifies the electronic item, its uncorrected OCR/TEI production, historical page-image delivery, and current NoC-US rights label.

evidence.citation: Carl Horstmann, ed., Sammlung altenglischer Legenden, grösstentheils zum ersten Male herausgegeben (Heilbronn: Gebr. Henninger, 1878), Boston Public Library copy digitized by Internet Archive.
evidence.locator: Title and dedication; printed pages 1–227; unnumbered contents page.
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the anthology boundary, component/version structure, printed metrics, manuscript sigla, source acknowledgments, and editorial routes.

evidence.citation: Horstmann 1878 source statements, headnotes, apparatus, and terminal contents, checked against current manuscript catalogs and facsimiles.
evidence.locator: Part I heading and seven chapter divisions; Part II headnotes for Kindheit, Canticum, Magdalena, Marina, Euphrosyne, Cristine, Dorothe, Erasmus, Disputisoun, Roberd, and appendix.
evidence.summary: Supports exact component-to-source scopes, mediated routes through Laing and the Smith/Kölbing/Furnivall transcripts, dual-version boundaries, the CUL Ii.4.9 exclusion, and the unresolved Roberd reading partition.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for sammlungaltengli1878hors.
evidence.summary: Identifies the Boston Public Library carrier, scan provenance, available derivatives, and absence of an item-level reuse license in the audited metadata.

evidence.citation: Gemeinsame Normdatei, Carl Horstmann, GND 1153444186.
evidence.locator: Preferred name Horstmann, Carl; birth year 1851; biographical note distinguishing the 1847 namesake; embedded CC0 license URI.
evidence.summary: Identifies the editor as the Carl Horstmann born in 1851 and distinguishes him from the namesake ophthalmologist; no death date is asserted for the editor.

evidence.citation: Theodor Grässe, ed., Jacobi a Voragine Legenda aurea, vulgo Historia Lombardica dicta (1846), Internet Archive scan.
evidence.summary: Confirms the Latin comparison edition cited for Part I; it is not a Middle English copy-text and contains none of the target anthology.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for LegendaAureaGraesse1846.
evidence.summary: Identifies the digitized 1846 comparison edition, derivative files, and Public Domain Mark.

evidence.citation: David Laing, ed., A Penni Worth of Witte (Edinburgh: Abbotsford Club, 1857), NLS digital copy.
evidence.summary: Provides the printed intermediary from EUL MS 218 through which Horstmann took Canticum version-b lines 1–352.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Catalogue of Medieval Manuscripts, MS. Eng. poet. a. 1 (Vernon Manuscript).
evidence.summary: Confirms the physical witness identity, date, contents, foliation, and target-text locations.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF Presentation manifest for the Vernon Manuscript, object 52f0a31a-1478-40e4-b05b-fddb1ad076ff.
evidence.summary: Confirms a complete 710-canvas digital carrier and exact canvas mappings for all Vernon target portions.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian, Terms of Use.
evidence.summary: States that most Digital Bodleian images are offered under CC BY-NC 4.0, subject to item-specific restrictions and provider conditions.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts catalog records for Arundel MS 168, Add MS 36983, and Harley MSS 525, 1701, 2253, 2382, 2399, 3954, and 5272.
evidence.locator: Ten JSON records listed with exact hashes under nine witness-specific British Library catalog access records; Harley MS 2253 has parent and Part II records.
evidence.summary: Confirms witness identities, dates, composite-part boundaries, contents, and target folio ranges; the catalog records contain no target text or images.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services, online ordering guidance and linked beta order form.
evidence.locator: Online ordering; required shelfmark, title, folios, format, payment, delivery, restrictions, and beta-service caveats.
evidence.summary: Confirms a fee-based request route for images from British Library collections. The service warns of possible delays and restrictions and does not guarantee that a specific manuscript order will be fulfilled.

evidence.citation: British Library IIIF Presentation manifest for Harley MS 3954.
evidence.summary: Confirms the complete 269-canvas published facsimile, target mapping ff. 70r–74r to canvases 147–155, and the geographically qualified public-domain statement.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Oxford MS 57 catalog description, hosted by the Bodleian Libraries.
evidence.summary: Confirms the witness identity, date, and current foliation: Canticum ff. 157v–165r and Roberd ff. 165r–167r; this corrects Horstmann's historical Canticum start reference.
notes: On 2026-07-16 the exact PDF route returned an Anubis verification page to direct retrieval and a 403 to an independent fetch. No access control was bypassed and no local PDF copy is claimed.

evidence.citation: University of Edinburgh Archives, MS 218 Auchinleck manuscript fragments.
evidence.summary: Confirms the detached Edinburgh fragment carrier and helps distinguish it from the main NLS codex and from other EUL MS 218 fragments.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland manuscript catalog, Advocates MS 19.2.1 (Auchinleck Manuscript).
evidence.summary: Confirms the physical NLS carrier, date, foliation, and relevant Canticum and Magdalena portions.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland IIIF Presentation manifest for Adv. MS 19.2.1, digital object 233337262.
evidence.summary: Confirms the complete 734-canvas current facsimile, exact target canvas mappings, and the Public Domain Mark 1.0 statement.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland, Auchinleck Manuscript project, complete published 701-image inventory.
evidence.summary: Confirms a project-level reconstruction spanning the NLS codex and detached fragments; target images split as NLS items 27–32 and 127–134 versus EUL MS 218 items 686–689.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland, Auchinleck Manuscript project, Copyright and use.
evidence.summary: Declares site materials copyrighted, permits personal use, and prohibits unauthorized commercial use or publication.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS. Laud Misc. 108 catalog TEI, pinned at medieval-mss commit cd36dce9a104617f960dab2c97ea78f95f917496.
evidence.summary: Confirms MS Laud Misc. 108, its date, contents, and Magdalena at ff. 190r–197r.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF Presentation manifest for MS Laud Misc. 108, object c5438c30-71dd-4cec-ac59-eb1e5a11f2a4.
evidence.summary: Confirms a complete 496-canvas digital carrier and maps ff. 190r–197r to canvases 395–409.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38.
evidence.summary: Supports the manuscript identity and Roberd of Cisyle location at ff. 254r–257v; no CUL item-level image surrogate was used.

evidence.citation: M. R. James, unpublished description of Cambridge University Library MS Ii.4.9, transcription by Jayne Ringrose, Cambridge University Library repository.
evidence.locator: King Robert of Sicily, ff. 87v–93v; repository rights metadata.
evidence.summary: Reconciles Horstmann's likely Cbr. Ji IV, 8 reference with current MS Ii.4.9 and supplies a catalog description only; Horstmann says the cited copy was unavailable, so no source relation is asserted.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.locator: Complete four-page April 2026 circular and duration tables.
evidence.summary: Supports the conservative U.S.-jurisdiction public-domain conclusions for nineteenth-century published editions and centuries-old manuscript content; provider licenses and terms are recorded separately.

evidence.citation: 17 U.S.C. § 105, Subject matter of copyright: United States Government works.
evidence.summary: Provides that U.S. copyright protection is not available for works of the United States Government and supports the U.S.-jurisdiction status assigned to the Copyright Office circular.

open_questions.question: Which physical copy supplied U-M's 234 historical page images, and is a currently auditable complete image route available?
next_steps: Inspect U-M digital-library administrative metadata or ask the repository whether the carrier barcode and complete current image route can be supplied.
notes: Until resolved, the U-M page-image entity remains distinct from the Boston Public Library/Internet Archive facsimile.

open_questions.question: Can the exact official Trinity College Oxford MS 57 catalog PDF be retrieved through a repository-supported route without bypassing the current verification challenge?
next_steps: Retry the exact official PDF endpoint later or ask Trinity College/Bodleian Libraries for a supported direct copy if the verification challenge persists.
notes: No local copy is recorded because the current response was HTML/403 rather than a verified PDF.

open_questions.question: Where do the Lucy Toulmin Smith, Eugen Kölbing, and F. J. Furnivall working transcripts now survive, if at all?
next_steps: Search personal-paper finding aids and correspondence catalogs for the named scholars, then contact plausible repositories if item-level records remain unavailable.
notes: Their intermediary role is explicit in the edition even though current repositories are unresolved.

open_questions.question: What exact line-by-line partition of Horstmann's Roberd of Cisyle derives from Vernon versus Trinity College Oxford MS 57?
next_steps: Collate the printed text and apparatus against complete images or diplomatic transcriptions of both principal witnesses.
notes: Both principal transcription routes are secure; only their exact reading partition is unresolved.

open_questions.question: Can Cristine's inferred Arundel MS 168 endpoint at f. 4v be confirmed directly?
next_steps: Inspect a complete Arundel MS 168 facsimile or a more granular item-level catalog description.
notes: The source witness is confirmed; only the endpoint is inferred from Dorothe's cataloged start at f. 5r.

open_questions.question: Can Horstmann's unavailable Cbr. Ji IV, 8 reference be proven to be current CUL MS Ii.4.9 rather than merely identified with high probability?
next_steps: Trace historical Cambridge shelfmark forms in nineteenth-century catalogs and compare Horstmann's description with the James record.
notes: Resolution would improve identifier history but would not add the witness to the 1878 edition source chain.

open_questions.question: Does the CME repository's Unlicense file intentionally apply to the corpus XML/data as well as software?
next_steps: Seek an explicit repository or publisher statement defining the licensed material scope.
notes: The manifest records the repository statement but leaves the artifact's item-level copyright status unknown.
